name: Ertapenem
executiveSummary: Sequential outpatient hospitalization treatment for intra-abdominal infections and complicated urinary tract infections.
indications:
## Main indications

Sequential outpatient hospitalization treatment for intra-abdominal infections and complicated urinary tract infections.

> Adjust according to institutional protocol and medical prescription.

adulto.presentation: Powder vial with lyophilized powder containing 1 g (Invanz, Ertapenem Richet).
adulto.reconstitution: 10 mL SWFI or NS. Conc: 100 mg/mL.
adulto.administration: IM: Yes. Reconst. with 3.2 mL painless solvent and administer deep IM. Intermittent IV: Yes. Reconst. with 10 mL SWFI or NS, dilute with 50-100 mL NS and infuse over 30-60 min.
adulto.notes: No more than 6 h should pass between reconstitution and end of administration. Dilution in 5% dextrose is not recommended due to instability.

pediatrico.dose: Intra-abdominal infections and complicated urinary tract infections: 3 months to 12 years: 15 mg/kg/dose every 12 h for 5 to 14 days, maximum 1 g/day; > 13 years and adults: 1 g for 14 days.
pediatrico.notes: Administer with caution in patients with brain lesions or history of seizures. IM: Reconstitute 1 g vial with 3.2 ml of 1% or 2% lidocaine, shake well before use (administer deep IM in a large muscle mass within one hour of preparation). IV: Shake well after reconstitution and dilute in normal saline to a final concentration of 20 mg/ml, infuse over 30 minutes; diluted solution is stable 6 h at room temperature and 24 h refrigerated. Probenecid decreases renal clearance of ertapenem. Contains 137 mg of sodium (approx. 6 mEq)/g of ertapenem.

stability:
## Reconstituted (in vial)

- 1 h refrigerated.

## Diluted solution (for administration)

- 6 h refrigerated.
adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

Abdominal pain, constipation, indigestion, nausea, vomiting, headache, vaginitis, seizures, injection-site pain.

bibliography.citation: Hospital de Pediatría SAMIC Prof. Dr. Juan P. Garrahan. Institutional Pharmaceutical Formulary — ERTApenem* (code 1703, ATC J01DH).
Infectious Diseases Service, Infection Prevention and Control. UCIP 2026 — Dilution and stability guide.
ANMAT. Drug information and authorized prescribing information in Argentina.
Argentine Society of Infectious Diseases (SADI). Guidelines and consensus statements.
